name: Kone Employees
description_short: KONE Employees Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative serving KONE employees in Moline, Illinois with credit union services including share accounts and member lending.
description_long: KONE Employees Credit Union (KONE ECU) is a federal credit union established to serve the financial needs of employees at KONE, a global engineering and technology company. As a member-owned, not-for-profit institution, it operates under the regulatory oversight of the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA), which insures member deposits similar to FDIC insurance at traditional banks. The credit union is headquartered in Moline, Illinois, reflecting its origins as an employee benefit organization. Credit unions like KONE ECU typically offer financial products designed around member benefit rather than profit maximization, which often results in competitive rates and lower fees compared to traditional banks. KONE ECU serves a defined membership base of KONE employees and potentially their families, creating a community-focused financial institution. The organization is listed through the National Credit Union Service Organization (NCUSO), a network that helps connect credit union members with services and information. While specific product details are limited in available information, credit unions generally provide share accounts (savings), share certificates (CDs), and member lending products including personal loans and payday alternative loans (PALs). KONE ECU's membership model means that members are technically owners, giving them voting rights on credit union governance and potential participation in earnings through dividends. The credit union's employee-focused structure suggests alignment with workplace benefits and payroll deduction options. As a smaller, specialized credit union, KONE ECU likely maintains personalized member service compared to large national institutions.
services: Payday alternative loans (PALs) - typical credit union product
In-person branch banking at Moline location

pros: Member-owned, not-for-profit structure typically offers lower fees and better rates than traditional banks
NCUA-insured deposits provide same protection as FDIC-insured bank accounts
Specifically designed to serve KONE employees, allowing alignment with workplace benefits and payroll integration
Local branch in Moline, Illinois provides in-person service and relationship banking
Credit union model means members participate in governance and potential earnings distribution
Access to credit union network services through NCUSO membership
Community-focused financial institution without shareholder profit pressures
cons: Membership restricted to KONE employees and potentially their families—not open to general public
Limited branch network with only one location identified in Moline, restricting convenient access for members in other areas
Website provides minimal product details, service offerings, and APR/rate information for comparison
Limited online presence makes it difficult to research services, hours, or complete application process
No information available about digital banking capabilities, mobile app, or remote account opening
best_for: Current KONE employees seeking payroll-integrated financial services and member-owner benefits
KONE employee families eligible for membership and wanting to consolidate finances at one institution
Members seeking relationship banking and personalized service in the Moline, Illinois area
Employees interested in payday alternative loans (PALs) as a credit-building alternative to payday lenders
diagnosis: KONE Employees Credit Union is best for current KONE employees in or near Moline, Illinois seeking member-owned banking with competitive rates and the ability to participate in credit union governance. The main caveat is that this is a restricted-membership credit union only available to KONE employees, and the limited online information makes it necessary to call (309) 736-6461 directly to evaluate specific products, rates, and eligibility.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
